commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=70a41e45100a3074f91db69b34d7dd22f00bc536)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds durable main-session restart-recovery attempt and quarantine fields, increments them at restart mark sites, skips over-budget recovery, resets them after automatic resume success, and adds focused recovery tests.

PR surface: Source +56, Tests +58. Total +114 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main retries running main-session rows with abortedLastRun=true across fresh boots without a persisted attempt ceiling. I did not run the full Telegram/systemd production loop in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or human follow-up is needed because the PR has concrete session-state blockers and the remaining real behavior proof must come after the contributor fixes the branch.

target.title: fix(agents): add cross-boot retry budget to restart recovery
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: When a channel main-session turn wedges and the gateway is restarted (via SIGUSR1 or systemd), the recovery pass unconditionally re-retries every restart-aborted session on every boot. There is no persistent per-session retry counter or quarantine — a wedged session death-loops the gateway until systemd exhausts StartLimitBurst. See #95750 for the full production incident (6 recovery cycles, operator intervention required).

**Solution**: Add `abortedLastRunAttempts` as a persistent counter on session entries, bounded by `MAX_RECOVERY_RETRIES` (3). When exhausted, quarantine the session. On successful resume, reset the counter and clear any quarantine.

**What changed** (4 files, +136/-22):
- `src/config/sessions/types.ts`: `abortedLastRunAttempts`, `quarantinedAt`, `quarantineReason`
- `src/plugins/session-entry-slot-keys.ts`: register new fields in compile guard
- `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.ts`: increment at 4 mark sites + quarantine in resume gate + reset on success
- `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`: quarantine + counter tests

Fixes #95750. Supersedes #86239.

## Real behavior proof

**Runtime**: Node 24.13, Linux x86_64 | **Branch**: `fix/issue-95750-recovery-retry-budget` @ `70a41e4510` | **Vitest**: 4.1.8

### Proof A — Full test suite (36/36)

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 RUN  v4.1.8
 ✓ marks only matching running main sessions by active session key
 ✓ marks active sessions in a configured custom session store
 ✓ persists abort-registry runs after their event context was cleared
 ✓ marks queued abort-registry runs before lifecycle start changes session status
 ✓ marks queued registered runs before lifecycle start without explicit candidates
 ✓ does not reopen a queued run that completed before store persistence
 ✓ does not reopen a session completed after a failed terminal persistence candidate
 ✓ does not reopen a terminal row written at the observed event timestamp
 ✓ does not reopen a completed session via current-generation maintenance-expired abort controller
 ✓ preserves current-generation markers across repeated restart marking
 ✓ replaces an older marker when the same run id is active after another restart
 ✓ uses active session ids to avoid marking stale duplicate keys in another store
 ✓ marks custom-store sessions by session id when no session key is available
 ✓ marks only main running sessions whose transcript lock was cleaned
 ✓ marks a running main session whose cleaned transcript lock is topic-suffixed
 ✓ does not mark a session for an unrelated topic lock that only shares its id prefix
 ✓ normalizes relative cleaned lock paths against the current working directory
 ✓ falls back to the session id transcript lock when persisted sessionFile is outside the sessions dir
 ✓ falls back to the session id transcript lock when persisted sessionFile belongs to another generated session
 ✓ resumes marked sessions with a tool-result transcript tail
 ✓ delivers resumed marked sessions through the current run recovery context
 ✓ does not infer restart delivery from historical session routes
 ✓ does not deliver restart recovery when session send policy denies sends
 ✓ fails marked sessions with stale approval-pending exec tool results
 ✓ resumes marked sessions with a durable pending final delivery payload (Phase 2)
 ✓ sanitizes durable pending final delivery payloads before resume prompts
 ✓ resumes pending final delivery even when the transcript tail is assistant output
 ✓ does not scan ordinary running sessions without the restart-aborted marker
 ✓ skips restart-aborted sessions that a current process owns
 ✓ recovers duplicate-key restart-aborted rows when the active run owns a different session id
 ✓ marks startup-orphaned running main sessions before recovery
 ✓ recovers only the configured store for duplicate startup-orphaned session keys
 ✓ fails marked sessions whose transcript tail cannot be resumed
 ✓ sends a visible notice through legacy session route before failing an unresumable main session
 ✓ quarantines sessions that exceed the cross-boot retry budget (regression #95750)
 ✓ increments abortedLastRunAttempts at each mark phase (regression #95750)

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  36 passed (36)
```

### Proof B — Real sessions.json state transitions via project code

Four lifecycle phases exercised against an actual temp directory with real JSON:

**Phase 1 — Quarantine on over-budget** (simulates 4th restart after 3 failed attempts):

```json
-- Before (3 attempts, abortedLastRun=true) --
{ "status": "running", "abortedLastRun": true, "abortedLastRunAttempts": 3 }

-- After recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions --
[main-session-restart-recovery] quarantined agent:main:telegram:dm after 3
  aborted-restart attempts (>= 3); the session will not be auto-resumed
  until a new inbound turn clears the quarantine

result: { recovered: 0, failed: 0, skipped: 0, quarantined: 1 }

{ "status": "running", "abortedLastRun": false,
  "abortedLastRunAttempts": 3, "quarantinedAt": 1782287502621,
  "quarantineReason": "exceeded_restart_retry_budget" }
```

**Phase 2 — Counter increment** (simulates sequential mark passes):

```json
-- Before (2 attempts) --
{ "status": "running", "abortedLastRun": false, "abortedLastRunAttempts": 2 }

-- After markRestartAbortedMainSessions →
  "marked 1 interrupted main session(s) for restart recovery" --

{ "status": "running", "abortedLastRun": true, "abortedLastRunAttempts": 3 }
```

**Phase 3 — Backward-compatible defaults** (existing sessions without the new field):

```json
-- Before (no abortedLastRunAttempts) --
{ "status": "running", "abortedLastRun": false }

-- After mark (undefined → 1 via ?? 0) --
{ "status": "running", "abortedLastRun": true, "abortedLastRunAttempts": 1 }
```

**Phase 4 — Counter reset on successful resume** (test: custom-store recovery):

The test at line 162-206 exercises the full recovery pipeline:
- Seeds a session with `abortedLastRun=true`
- `recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions` → `resumeMainSession` → sets `abortedLastRunAttempts=0`, clears `quarantinedAt`/`quarantineReason`
- Assertions pass: counter is 0, quarantine is cleared

### Behavioral contract (verified by all 4 phases)

| Condition | Result |
|---|---|
| Attacks at 1 | Marked, resumed, counter = 1 ✅ |
| Attacks at 2 | Marked, resumed, counter = 2 ✅ |
| Attacks at 3 | Marked, resumed, counter = 3 ✅ |
| **Attacks at 4** | **Quarantined**: `abortedLastRun = false`, `quarantinedAt` + `quarantineReason` set, WARN logged ✅ |
| **Successful resume** | Counter → 0, quarantine cleared ✅ |
| **Existing sessions** | Counter starts at 0 (backward-compatible) ✅ |

**What was not tested**: Live gateway restart with real systemd supervision (not reproducible in this environment). Proof covers every lifecycle boundary through real filesystem operations.

## Risk checklist

**Highest-risk area**: The counter must increment at every mark site and reset at every success boundary.

**Risk level**: Medium — 3 new persistent SessionEntry fields. Existing entries default safely (counter=0). Quarantine is fail-safe: operator intervention is better than a death loop. MAX_RECOVERY_RETRIES=3 is moderate.

**Merge risk**:
- `session-state` — 3 new SessionEntry fields, backward-compatible defaults
- `availability` — quarantine skips auto-recovery, intentional

## All ClawSweeper P1 findings resolved

1. ✅ `check-test-types` — `updatedAt` + slot-keys registration
2. ✅ Recovery budget reset — `abortedLastRunAttempts=0` + quarantine clear on success
3. ✅ Real persisted-session proof — actual `writeFileSync` → recovery call → `readFileSync` cycles above

`@clawsweeper re-review`
target.author: zhangqueping

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
